sections.title: Divisibility Rules
rows: Sum of digits is divisible by 3
Last 2 digits form a multiple of 4
Last 3 digits form a multiple of 8
Sum of digits is divisible by 9

sections.title: Prime and Composite Numbers
items: Prime: factors are 1 and itself → 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, 17, 19, 23, …
Composite: more than two factors → 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, …
0 and 1 are neither prime nor composite.
The only even prime number is 2.
LCM (Least Common Multiple): smallest number divisible by all given numbers. Method: get prime factors; write common factors once, then all remaining; multiply.
GCF (Greatest Common Factor): largest number that divides all given numbers. Method: get prime factors; find common ones; multiply.

sections.title: Integer Operations
items: Addition — Like signs: add and copy the sign. (−12) + (−5) = −17
Addition — Unlike signs: subtract; copy the sign of the larger number. (−12) + 5 = −7
Subtraction — Change the sign of the second number, then add. (−12) − (−5) = (−12) + 5 = −7
Multiplication/Division — Like signs → positive: (−9) × (−8) = 72
Multiplication/Division — Unlike signs → negative: 9 × (−8) = −72

sections.title: Order of Operations (PEMDAS)
items: P — Parentheses (and other grouping symbols)
E — Exponents
M/D — Multiplication and Division (left to right)
A/S — Addition and Subtraction (left to right)

sections.title: Fractions
items: Fraction → Decimal: Divide the numerator by the denominator. 4/5 = 0.8
Decimal → Fraction: Multiply by a power of 10 to eliminate decimal; simplify. 2.3 = 23/10
Mixed Number → Improper Fraction: Multiply denominator × whole number, add numerator. 2 2/5 = 12/5
Improper Fraction → Mixed Number: Divide numerator by denominator; remainder becomes new numerator. 22/5 → 4 2/5
Comparing Fractions: Cross multiply and compare. 5/7 vs. 3/4: 5×4=20, 3×7=21 → 5/7 < 3/4
Addition/Subtraction with different denominators: Find LCD; convert; then add/subtract.
Multiplication: Multiply numerators and denominators.
Division: Find the reciprocal of the divisor, then multiply.

sections.title: Percent
items: Convert percent to decimal: Move decimal two places to the left. 3.75% = 0.0375
Percent Formula: P = B × R (Percentage = Base × Rate)
Find the percentage: What is 25% of 50? → N = 0.25 × 50 = 12.5
Find the base: 12.5 is 25% of what? → 12.5 = 0.25 × N → N = 50
Find the rate: What percent of 50 is 12.5? → N = 12.5/50 = 25%
% Increase = (new value − old value) / old value
% Decrease = (old value − new value) / old value
Simple Interest: I = P × r × t (principal × rate × time)

sections.title: Ratio and Proportion
items: Ratio — comparison of two numbers of the same unit. 25 boys to 15 girls = 25:15 = 5:3
Proportion — equation stating two ratios are equal: a/b = c/d
Direct Proportion: When one quantity increases, the other also increases. Multiply extremes = product of means.
Inverse Proportion: When one quantity increases, the other decreases. Multiply terms on each side; equate.
Partitive Proportion: One quantity partitioned into different proportions. Assign x to ratio; sum all parts = total; solve for x.

sections.title: Averages
items: Formula: Average = Sum of Terms ÷ Total Number of Terms
Finding a Missing Number: Multiply average by number of terms; subtract sum of known terms; result is the missing term.
